name: QuixLab
shortDescription: Reactive notebook on a collaborative canvas — explore the lakehouse, live Kafka topics, and blob storage from Python cells with interactive widgets and rich visualizations.
longDescription:
QuixLab combines the reactive cell model of Marimo with a spatial, Miro-style collaboration canvas. Drop dataset, stream, cell, and widget nodes anywhere, wire them together by argument name, and the dependency graph re-executes automatically when any input changes.

Three first-class data sources are wired straight into the Quix data plane:
• Lakehouse — run SQL against QuixLake with ql.sql("…") and get a DataFrame back.
• Kafka topics — subscribe to a live stream with ql.topic("name") and read from a rolling buffer in real time.
• Blob storage — read and write files or folders as first-class variables via StorageFile / StorageFolder.

Visualize results inline with one-line plots (table, line, bar, scatter, pie, heatmap), full-power Plotly and Matplotlib figures, or rendered Markdown. Add reactive widgets (text input, dropdown, button) that automatically re-run downstream cells when their values change — perfect for parameterized SQL queries and live dashboards.

Notebooks are stored as plain Python files that run standalone with python notebook.py; layout, viz config and dependencies are persisted as decorator keyword arguments, so the file stays diff-friendly and Git-trackable.

The deployment ships with a 1 GB persistent state volume for your notebooks, auto-binds the workspace's blob storage, and is embedded directly in the Quix UI as a top-level plugin so anyone in the workspace can land on the canvas from the sidebar.

Variables.Name: QUIXLAB_NOTEBOOK
Variables.Description: Notebook file to open on startup (leave empty for the file picker)
